qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Shell command allowlist bypass enables arbitrary command execution via WebSocket
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns a WebSocket command execution allowlist bypass in http/commands.go and runner/parser.go, while the advisory concerns public share path traversal in http/public.go caused by using filepath.Dir(link.Path) for directory share roots.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described public share path traversal root cause. The target advisory is about directory share links escaping to the parent directory in http/public.go when withHashFile uses filepath.Dir(link.Path); the only qualifying finding is an authenticated WebSocket shell command allowlist bypass.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-mr74-928f-rw69 describes public share directory traversal through http/public.go withHashFile, where filepath.Dir(link.Path) incorrectly roots BasePathFs at the shared directory parent.
The shell_command_injection finding identifies http/commands.go and runner/parser.go, where the first token is allowlisted but the raw command string is passed to a configured shell.

The finding's affected entrypoint is WebSocket /api/command{path:.*}; the advisory's affected entrypoints are public share listing and download endpoints under /api/public/share/{hash} and /api/public/dl/{hash}/path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
